summary: Tested all 3 new features: Stripe checkout flow, Email verification, and DJ Vedette (featured). All 16 backend tests passed. Frontend pages verified. Fixed 2 bugs: missing email_service import in register endpoint, and auth guard blocking verify-email/feature-success pages for unauthenticated users. DJ Vedette badge and sorting confirmed working.

minor.issue: email_service was not imported in the register function (line ~394) - caused 'name email_service is not defined' error. Fixed by adding 'from services.email_service import email_service' inside the try block.
minor.priority: MEDIUM (non-blocking - caught by try/except, registration still works but email won't be sent)

ui_bugs.issue: verify-email and payment/feature-success routes were not registered in Stack.Screen list AND not excluded from auth redirect. Both pages were showing 'Unmatched Route' or redirecting to login for unauthenticated users. FIXED.

issues: Badge displays correctly with gold star and 'VEDETTE' text on top-left of card image. Visual design is clean and visible.

action_items: IMPORTANT: CI=true in expo environment means any frontend file changes require clearing .metro-cache and restarting expo to take effect. This was confirmed again in this iteration.
The _layout.tsx isPublicRoute fix is critical for the email verification UX - users click email links while unauthenticated. Main agent should review this fix.

Minor: feature-success page calls /api/dj/feature/status which requires auth - but this page is intended for logged-in DJs who just paid, so auth requirement is acceptable. However if the page loads before auth is set, it may briefly show 'Erreur de paiement'.
DJ TestPrice was set to is_featured=true in the database for testing purposes. Should be reset or left for demo purposes.
critical_code_review_comments: email_service import was missing in register endpoint (line 394) - fixed in this iteration. The password reset endpoint had the correct pattern (lazy import inside function).

verify-email and payment/feature-success routes were missing from _layout.tsx Stack.Screen list AND from the isPublicRoute check. Without isPublicRoute exception, unauthenticated users clicking email verification links get redirected to login instead of seeing the verification page.

The ORIGIN_URL in wallet.tsx is hardcoded to 'https://dj-connect-12.preview.emergentagent.com' instead of using process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. This works for the current deployment but would break in other environments.
seed_data_creation: Set testprice@test.com DJ profile (id: 97298b16-d099-4b7a-900d-b7d702603173) to is_featured=true with featured_until=30 days from now for testing the VEDETTE badge display.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new features work correctly: Stripe checkout (booking + featured DJ), email verification flow, verify-email page, feature-success page, VEDETTE badge on DJCard, featured DJ sorting. Fixed: email_service import in register, _layout.tsx auth guard for public routes. DJ TestPrice is currently marked as featured in DB. The CI=true mode in expo requires cache clear + restart for any frontend source changes to take effect.

email_service_missing_import.root_cause: The register endpoint at line 394 calls email_service.send_email_verification() without importing email_service. The forgot-password endpoint correctly uses a lazy import (from services.email_service import email_service) inside the function, but this pattern wasn't followed in register.
email_service_missing_import.reproduction_steps: 1. Register a new user → backend logs show 'Could not send verification email: name email_service is not defined'
email_service_missing_import.mitigation: Added 'from services.email_service import email_service' inside the try block before the send call.

verify_email_auth_redirect.root_cause: 1) verify-email.tsx and payment/feature-success.tsx were not listed in Stack.Screen in _layout.tsx, causing 'Unmatched Route'. 2) Even after adding routes, the auth guard redirected unauthenticated users to login before they could see the verification result. 3) In CI mode, expo metro doesn't watch files, requiring cache clear + restart.
verify_email_auth_redirect.reproduction_steps: 1. Click email verification link while not logged in → 'Unmatched Route' (before fix) or redirect to login (after route fix but before isPublicRoute fix)
verify_email_auth_redirect.mitigation: Added routes to Stack.Screen list AND added isPublicRoute check using both segments[0] and pathname.startsWith() checks. Expo restarted to pick up changes.
